aboutNia.heading: About NIA
aboutNia.subtitle: The aims and objectives of the Association are:
aboutNia.objective1: To promote the friendship, knowledge and understanding of the culture, history, philosophy, religions and social structure of India and the Netherlands between the people of both countries.
aboutNia.objective2: To further friendly relations between the peoples of India and the Netherlands by widening and deepening personal, cultural and social contacts.

membership.heading: Membership
membership.intro: Individuals and families who agree with the objectives of the Association can become members by filling in the membership form and paying the annual contribution.

patron.perk: 🎟️ Free Entry to All NIA Events

infoCard.line1: The Executive Board plans and organizes activities that support our mission and bring our communities together.
infoCard.line2: Members guide our direction through the General Meeting and active participation.

assocStructure.heading: Structure of the Association
assocStructure.card1: Honorary membership may be conferred upon individuals who have made an outstanding contribution to Indo-Dutch cultural cooperation.
assocStructure.card2: The Association is managed by an Executive Board. The General Meeting consists of members. The Executive Board comprises the office bearers: President, Vice-President, Secretary and Treasurer.

activities.heading: Activities
activities.subtitle:
The Association organizes about four to five activities every year, sometimes in cooperation with other organizations.
These activities bring the Dutch and Indian communities together in the Netherlands.

intro.heading: About the Netherlands India Association
intro.body: The Netherlands India Association (NIA) is a vibrant, non-profit organisation dedicated to nurturing the bond between the Dutch and Indian communities. Founded on a shared passion for cultural exchange, we bring together individuals, families, and businesses who celebrate the richness of Indo-Dutch heritage.

mission.heading: What Drives Us
mission.intro: The Netherlands India Association is built on a foundation of cultural pride, human connection, and a belief that shared experiences make the world a richer place.

culturalBridge.title: Cultural Bridge
culturalBridge.desc: We connect two great civilisations — Dutch and Indian — through art, music, cuisine, festivals, and shared stories that span centuries.

communityFirst.title: Community First
communityFirst.desc: Every event, initiative, and programme we run is designed to bring people together and strengthen the bonds within our community.

mutualRespect.title: Mutual Respect
mutualRespect.desc: We celebrate diversity and promote understanding between cultures, honouring both the Dutch and Indian ways of life equally.

inspireEducate.title: Inspire & Educate
inspireEducate.desc: From cultural workshops to heritage talks, we create meaningful experiences that inspire curiosity and deepen appreciation for both nations.

structure.honoraryText: Honorary membership may be conferred upon individuals who have made an outstanding contribution to Indo-Dutch cultural cooperation. It is the highest distinction the Association can bestow, recognising exceptional service and dedication.

cta.body: Want to be part of a community that celebrates two great cultures? Become a member of the Netherlands India Association today.
cta.subPrefix: Open to everyone who shares a love for Indo-Dutch friendship —
cta.subStrong: join us and make a difference.

about.heading: About The Event
about.body1: Join us for a historic celebration as we mark India's 80th Independence Day and the 75th Anniversary of NIA.
about.body2: An evening filled with cultural performances, delicious cuisine, inspiring moments and togetherness as we honour our heritage and strengthen the bond between two nations.

community and friendship.

cookieConsent.title: Cookie notice
cookieConsent.body: We use cookies and similar storage technologies to run this site and, with your permission, to understand how it's used. Choose your preferences below.

modal.title: Cookie Preferences
modal.intro: We use different types of cookies and similar storage technologies to run this website. Choose which categories to allow below — you can change your mind at any time via "Cookie Settings" in the footer.

necessary.name: Strictly Necessary
necessary.desc: Required for the site to function — keeping you signed in, remembering your cookie choice, and security. These can't be switched off.

functional.name: Functional
functional.desc: Remembers your preferences, such as your chosen language (English or Dutch), for a more personalised experience.

analytics.name: Analytics
analytics.desc: Would help us understand how visitors use this site, so we can improve it. We don't currently use any analytics tools — this is here so you're in control if that ever changes.

marketing.name: Marketing
marketing.desc: Would be used to measure the effectiveness of any future advertising. We don't currently run any marketing or advertising cookies — this is here so you're in control if that ever changes.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy & Cookie Policy
privacyPolicy.intro: The Netherlands India Association (NIA) respects your privacy. This page explains what information we collect when you use this website, why we collect it, and the choices and rights you have.

whoWeAre.heading: Who we are
whoWeAre.body: The Netherlands India Association (NIA) is a non-profit cultural and community organisation based at Burg. Patijnlaan 1062, 2585 CB Den Haag, the Netherlands. For any privacy-related question, you can reach us at secretary@niaonline.org.

dataWeCollect.heading: What information we collect
dataWeCollect.body:
We collect the information you provide directly to us, such as when you:

• Register for a membership, sign in to the member dashboard, or update your profile — name, email address, phone number, and membership details.
• Buy event tickets, make a donation, or apply for sponsorship — name, email address, phone number, and (for sponsorships) organisation details.
• Contact us directly by email.

We do not collect sensitive personal data (such as health, religious, or political information) and we do not knowingly collect information from children without a parent or guardian's involvement.

howWeUseIt.heading: How we use your information
howWeUseIt.body: We use the information we collect to: create and manage your membership or member account; process ticket, donation, and sponsorship payments; send you confirmations, receipts, and membership or event-related communications; and respond to questions you send us directly. We do not sell your personal information, and we do not use it for automated profiling or targeted advertising.

cookies.heading: Cookies and similar technologies
cookies.body:
When you first visit this site, we ask you to choose which categories of cookies and similar storage technologies (like your browser's local storage) you're comfortable with:

• Strictly Necessary — always on. Keeps you signed in, remembers your cookie choice, and supports security. The site can't function without these.
• Functional — remembers your chosen language (English or Dutch) for a more personalised experience.
• Analytics and Marketing — categories we've built in for the future. We do not currently use any analytics or advertising tools of any kind, so choosing to allow these categories has no effect today. If that ever changes, we will only start using them for visitors who have opted in, and will update this policy first.

You can change your choice at any time using the "Cookie Settings" link in the footer of every page.

thirdParties.heading: Payment processing
thirdParties.body: Ticket, membership, donation, and sponsorship payments are processed by Mollie B.V., a licensed Dutch payment service provider. When you pay through this website, your payment details (such as card or iDEAL information) are handled directly by Mollie under its own privacy and security policies — NIA does not receive or store your full payment card details.

dataRetention.heading: How long we keep your information
dataRetention.body: We keep membership and transaction records for as long as your membership is active, and afterwards for as long as required by Dutch accounting and tax obligations. If you ask us to delete your account, we will do so except where we're legally required to retain certain records (for example, financial records related to a completed payment).

yourRights.heading: Your rights
yourRights.body: Under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have the right to: access the personal information we hold about you; ask us to correct inaccurate information; ask us to delete your information; restrict or object to how we use it; and receive a copy of your information in a portable format. To exercise any of these rights, email secretary@niaonline.org. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Dutch Data Protection Authority (Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens) if you believe we have not handled your information properly.

changes.heading: Changes to this policy
changes.body: We may update this policy from time to time, for example if we start using new tools on the website. We'll update the date at the top of this page whenever we make a meaningful change.

contact.heading: Contact us
contact.body: If you have any questions about this policy or how we handle your information, please contact us at secretary@niaonline.org or by post at Burg. Patijnlaan 1062, 2585 CB Den Haag, the Netherlands.
